lenses.guidance: Five elements — incense altar, census tax, bronze basin, anointing oil, incense recipe — each gets its own short section. The narrative structure of repeated 'the LORD said to Moses' (vv 11, 17, 22, 34) marks five distinct words.

The half-shekel atonement-money (vv 11-16) is 'the same for rich and poor' (v 15) — covenant atonement levels the human field. The pattern points to Christ's ransom: one price, every soul, no scaling.
The incense altar (vv 1-10) carries prayer-imagery across Scripture — Ps 141:2 ('let my prayer be incense'), Lk 1:10's prayer at the hour of incense, Rev 5:8's prayers as incense before the throne.
